alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight years scaling global commercial operations and transformation programs at Google Devices and Services; demonstrated ability to align cross-functional stakeholders and drive strategic initiatives.
alignments.job_requirement: Lead deal strategy: Define win themes, stakeholder maps, and executive alignment for complex, multi-threaded pursuits.

alignments.candidate_evidence: Advanced Excel and Google Sheets; SQL and BigQuery proficiency for own data shaping; Salesforce admin with territory-budget management experience.
alignments.job_requirement: Structure deal economics: Build ROI/TCO, pricing architectures (usage/SaaS/hybrid), and commercial constructs; partner with Deal Desk, Legal, and Risk.

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product Operations and Strategy expertise; experience translating requirements into clear use cases and transformation programs; documented process enablement at scale.
alignments.job_requirement: Codify 'how we win': Produce playbooks, battlecards, competitor tear-downs, and vertical white papers; enable Enterprise AEs and regions.

alignments.candidate_evidence: Product Operations background; demonstrated ability to translate market insight and requirements into product strategy and use-case definition.
alignments.job_requirement: Inform product & roadmap: Synthesize enterprise requirements and market trends; translate into clear use cases and prioritization for Product.

alignments.candidate_evidence: Eight-year tenure with two promotions at a major technology company; demonstrated ability to scale operations and lead transformation across global functions.
alignments.job_requirement: Drive executive engagement: Lead C-suite briefings, POVs, and workshops; establish trusted relationships with business and technical stakeholders.

alignments.candidate_evidence: Basic Python and actively learning; proficient SQL and BigQuery querying; designs and directs LLM coding agents to understand system architecture and technical constraints.
alignments.job_requirement: Technical fluency (not a pure Solution Architect): Acquiring, issuing, wallets/treasury/FX; comfortable engaging at an architectural level to ensure solution fit.

gaps.gap: No stated payments, fintech, treasury, FX, or financial-services domain knowledge.
gaps.mitigation: Domain is learnable; candidate has strong operations and strategic framework experience. Airwallex' enterprise customers (Brex, Navan, SHEIN) operate in adjacent B2B/fintech, and candidate's ability to synthesize market and product insight should transfer. Ramp may require 2–3 months of immersion.

gaps.gap: Limited pre-sales consulting or hands-on solution architecture in an enterprise/consulting context.
gaps.mitigation: Candidate has driven solution-fit alignment internally (codev, UAT, rollout) and directed technical teams on AI tooling; framework and stakeholder-management skills are translatable. RFP orchestration and Deal Desk partnership can be learned on the job under mentorship.

gaps.gap: No stated experience with complex multi-product pricing architectures, usage-based or SaaS hybrid models, or ROI/TCO modeling at enterprise scale.
gaps.mitigation: Advanced Excel/Sheets and SQL proficiency are foundational; pricing logic and ROI frameworks are analytically tractable. Candidate has managed territory budgets in Salesforce; extension to deal-level commercial structuring is incremental.

gaps.gap: Consulting brand and case-team experience absent; candidate's strategy work is internal to Google, not advisory.
gaps.mitigation: External problem-solving mindset and founder-like independence cited as Airwallex values; candidate's Product Ops transformation and cross-functional orchestration demonstrate similar thinking. Not a hard blocker.

output.level_rationale: 8–12+ years required. "Senior Manager" title. enterprise GTM and strategy scope. reporting to Global Revenue Strategy department suggests senior individual-contributor or frontline-manager role. Company stage ($8B, 2,200+ headcount) and complexity anchor at L5 (established senior IC / lead role). no explicit manager-of-managers language.
output.summary: Strong role-family alignment: Strategy & Operations, GTM, and enterprise commercial leadership map directly to candidate's primary families. Deal strategy, RFP orchestration, and playbook codification match core Product Ops and commercial-strategy expertise. Material gaps: no domain expertise in payments, fintech, or treasury/FX; minimal pre-sales consulting or technical architecture experience relative to "technical fluency" requirement. Candidate's eight-year tenure and promotion history support seniority fit, but lack of payments-domain knowledge and hands-on solution architecture in consulting or post-sales presales create manageable but real onboarding friction.
uncertainties: Company stage and function-team size not stated in JD; 'Global Revenue Strategy department' suggests established enterprise GTM org, but depth and seniority delegation unknown.
Extent of candidate's direct C-suite exposure and executive presence at Google not detailed in profile; claimed 'established relationships with decision makers at all levels' requires verification.

Whether candidate has seen or modeled complex multi-customer contract structures, variable pricing, or deal-desk negotiations; territory budgets in Salesforce suggest process familiarity but not necessarily commercial negotiation depth.

Candidate's hands-on product-solution design is stated as 'ownership, requirements, logic design, UAT, rollout'; unclear whether this translates to credible technical fluency for APAC enterprise architecture discussions in payments context.
